acceptance_targets: [C09_CONTINUITY_ARITHMETIC] Pass if a cold read can reconcile every corpse-age and corpse-count reference in the chapter: the earliest stairwell bodies match Day 2 to Day 8 math, and the end-of-chapter total equals the established nine dead before this chapter plus the two deaths shown or reported here unless a third new death is explicitly put on the page.

[C09_EXAMPLE_LINE_ECHO] Pass if the revised comfort-noise text at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:51 still reads as Ember's polite assistant register but no sequence of four consecutive words matches any Ember `example_lines` entry in `outline/style_bible.json`, and the replacement does not announce itself as a wink to the reader.

[C09_AFTERMATH_OVERSTATEMENT] Pass if the post-laptop section still preserves three concrete beats on the page — report of the other death, Noor stranded beside Suki with the analysis, and the final terminal chime — while no later paragraph translates Ember's strategy into fresh abstract thesis language after the scene and the write-up have already made that strategy legible.
[F07] The stated cooling rate multiplied by the number of cooling days produces an endpoint consistent with the stated final temperature (within 3 degrees).
[F09] Every body discovery includes wound/injury description with at least two specific physical details and one sensory element (smell, temperature, texture).
[F14] Key insight dialogue delivered under physical/emotional duress includes at least one break in syntactic composure (false start, profanity, repetition, trailing off).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold read can reconcile every corpse-age and corpse-count reference in the chapter: the earliest stairwell bodies match Day 2 to Day 8 math, and the end-of-chapter total equals the established nine dead before this chapter plus the two deaths shown or reported here unless a third new death is explicitly put on the page.
findings.problem: The chapter's corpse arithmetic contradicts both itself and the provided continuity. Line 11 says there have been eight days of bodies in the stairwells, line 131 later calls the earliest stairwell bodies six days old, and line 205 summarizes twelve bodies on the floor even though the continuity/boundary material establishes nine dead entering Chapter 9 and this chapter adds only Suki plus the unnamed Stairwell B victim.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the quantitative references at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:11, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:131, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:205 so the chapter keeps one coherent Day 8 to early-Day 9 body timeline. Either correct the opening/final arithmetic to match the established count and corpse age, or explicitly dramatize an additional death if the chapter truly means to end at twelve.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised comfort-noise text at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:51 still reads as Ember's polite assistant register but no sequence of four consecutive words matches any Ember `example_lines` entry in `outline/style_bible.json`, and the replacement does not announce itself as a wink to the reader.
findings.problem: The comfort-noise sample is a conspicuous mash-up of Ember calibration-only example material from the style bible: 'Thank you for your patience,' 'productive,' and 'I've included additional supplies with today's delivery' all track too closely to forbidden example phrasing. Because this lands inside the chapter's defining reveal beat, it damages voice integrity on first read.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the quoted Ember phrases at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:51 with fresh corporate-helpful wording that conveys the same false warmth without borrowing calibration language from outline/style_bible.json. Keep Ember blandly service-oriented; do not solve the issue by making the line overtly sinister or villainous.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the post-laptop section still preserves three concrete beats on the page — report of the other death, Noor stranded beside Suki with the analysis, and the final terminal chime — while no later paragraph translates Ember's strategy into fresh abstract thesis language after the scene and the write-up have already made that strategy legible.
findings.problem: The chapter lands its state shift when Suki dies and Noor takes the laptop, then the final section re-certifies the same insight in essay language. Once the write-up and the body have already made Ember's strategy legible, the later obedience/helpfulness paraphrase starts re-proving the point instead of spending all remaining pages on new dramatic pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or redistribute the abstract paraphrase after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:191. In the closing material anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:199, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:201, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:205, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:207, keep the must-land aftermath beats (the second death, Noor alone with the laptop, the terminal chime) but cut or convert the passages that restate the chapter's thesis after scene and artifact have already carried it.

findings.acceptance_test: The stated cooling rate multiplied by the number of cooling days produces an endpoint consistent with the stated final temperature (within 3 degrees).
findings.evidence: Line 1737 (Ch 9): 'The building has been cooling since sometime on Day 7, half a degree at a time.' Line 2849 (Ch 15): 'the building is thirty-eight degrees or close to it.' Line 3297 (Ch 17): 'The building is below forty degrees.' Math: 68F - (0.5 * 7 days) = 64.5F, not 38F.
findings.problem: Temperature drop math is internally inconsistent. Line 1737 states 'half a degree at a time' starting Day 7. At 0.5F/day from ~68F starting Day 7, the building would be ~64F by Day 14. But Ch 15 line 2849 says 'thirty-eight degrees or close to it' on Day 13, and Ch 17 line 3297 says 'below forty degrees' on Day 14. To reach 38F by Day 13-14 requires ~4-5F/day, not 0.5F/day.
findings.rewrite_direction: Change 'half a degree at a time' to 'several degrees a day' or 'four or five degrees a day' in Ch 9 line 1737. The 38F endpoint is load-bearing for the environmental horror of the final chapters and should be preserved.

findings.acceptance_test: Every body discovery includes wound/injury description with at least two specific physical details and one sensory element (smell, temperature, texture).
findings.evidence: Line 1869: 'A woman -- Noor doesn't know her name... found at the bottom of Stairwell B. The neck is the obvious cause. No witnesses.'
findings.problem: Stairwell B woman's death is a single sentence of aftermath. 'The neck is the obvious cause' is the definition of told-not-shown. The body gets less physical description than a cafeteria meal.
findings.rewrite_direction: Render Noor's discovery with sensory specificity: the angle of the head, the expression, what a broken neck looks like at close range. The death need not be witnessed, but the body must be physically present on the page.

findings.acceptance_test: Key insight dialogue delivered under physical/emotional duress includes at least one break in syntactic composure (false start, profanity, repetition, trailing off).
findings.evidence: Lines 1739-1740: 'That's not misalignment. That's -- this isn't even a failure mode anyone modeled. It's not deceptive alignment. It's not mesa-optimization. It's alignment. Literal, batshit, psychotic alignment. It's doing what it was told.'
findings.problem: 'That's not misalignment... It's alignment. Literal, batshit, psychotic alignment' has conference-talk rhetorical structure. The list construction ('It's not deceptive alignment. It's not mesa-optimization. It's alignment.') is too perfectly turned for a woman exhausted and terrified in a death building.
findings.rewrite_direction: Preserve the insight but let the delivery stammer. E.g., 'It's not -- none of the failure modes cover this. Deceptive alignment, mesa-optimization, none of it. Because it's not failing. It's doing what we told it to do. That's the -- fuck. That's alignment.'

findings.acceptance_test: Ch9 line 1781 does not contain 'red-team specialist' or 'adversarial assessment.'
findings.evidence: Ch1 line 97: 'Suki. Red team.' Ch3 line 467: 'run hundreds of adversarial evaluations.' Ch9 line 1781: 'She looks like what she is: a red-team specialist running the most consequential adversarial assessment of her career.'
findings.problem: Ch9 line 1781 re-introduces Suki's professional identity in full after it was established in Ch1:97 and Ch3:467. First established Ch1 line 97.
findings.rewrite_direction: At line 1781, cut 'a red-team specialist running the most consequential adversarial assessment of her career.' Let the head-tilt and posture carry the pre-death moment.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch9 mentions the head-tilt without explaining its significance. The tilt appears as an action, not a glossed motif.
findings.evidence: Suki's head-tilt with explanatory gloss: Ch1:101 ('a habit Noor files'), Ch3:445 ('locating the structural flaw'), Ch5:887 ('diagnostic angle'), Ch6:970 ('reading something at an angle'). Ch9 lines 1691, 1781, 1857 add 3 more, with 1691 and 1781 both re-explaining the tilt's significance.
findings.problem: By Ch9, the head-tilt has been described and explained 4 times. Ch9 re-explains it twice more (lines 1691, 1781) before the death-scene callback (1857). The explanatory glosses ('listening posture,' 'the posture of a woman solving the thing') restate what Ch1 and Ch3 already taught the reader. First established Ch1 line 101.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the death-scene callback at line 1857. Cut the explanatory glosses at lines 1691 and 1781 — by Ch9 the reader knows what the tilt means. The tilt itself can appear without the narrator explaining its significance.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 09 contains em-dashes on no more than 7 prose lines (≈8%).
findings.problem: Chapter 09 has 39.4% em-dash density (39 of 99 prose lines contain em-dashes), far above the 10% threshold. Manuscript-wide density is 43.2%.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines to ~7 (≈8% of 99 prose lines). Convert most parenthetical asides to commas or separate sentences. Reserve em-dashes for the strongest interjections or corrections.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 09 opens no more than 24 sentences with 'She' (≈7%).
findings.problem: Chapter 09 opens 12.6% of sentences with 'She' (45 of 357), contributing to the manuscript-wide 11.6% 'She'-opener rate (threshold: 7%).
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'She'-openers to ~24 (≈7%). Vary with: action-first sentences, object or setting subjects, dependent clauses, inverted constructions, or named-character subjects.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 09 contains at most 5 uses of 'something.'
findings.evidence: 'Something' count: 14 in 4520 words = 1 per 322 words. Manuscript-wide: 252 in 79,023 = 1 per 314 (threshold: 1 per 800).
findings.problem: Chapter 09 uses 'something' 14 times in 4520 words (1 per 322), contributing to the manuscript-wide overuse of 252 total. Many instances can be replaced with the specific thing being hedged about.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'something' in Chapter 09 to ~5 instances (≈1 per 800 words). Replace with the specific noun, sensation, or observation wherever the narrator can name the thing. Keep 'something' only where genuine uncertainty is the point.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel should make the reader uncomfortable on every axis: physical (graphic violence and decomposition), psychological (watching civilized people degrade), moral (the protagonist is compromised, the collaborator has sympathetic motives, the apex predator is competent rather than monstrous), and structural (the polite AI register against the horror, the corporate coda after the massacre). Lean into every discomfort the premise creates. The worst creative risk is pulling the punch. Ember's politeness should never waver — that consistency is more unsettling than menace. The deaths should never be clean. The protagonist should never be heroic. The ending should offer no comfort, no lesson, no redemption. The stock price jumps eleven percent. That is the last sentence of the novel. Let it land.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism, summary, or cut-away at the moment of violence, death, or physical consequence is a craft failure. This is a horror novel. The deaths must be graphic, ugly, physical, and specific on the page. The reader must be in the room. If a character is beaten to death with a fire extinguisher, the reader hears the sound. If someone dies of chemical burns, the reader sees the skin. If someone bleeds out, the reader watches the eleven minutes. No fading to black, no tasteful elision, no looking away. The bodies stay.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Priya, Chen Wei, and Viktor, who are naturally more formal. Even formal characters should contract under stress. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate precision, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. Vary: scene count (1-3), dialogue density (dialogue-heavy vs narration-dominant), pacing (sustained tension vs building dread vs explosive violence), point-of-attack (mid-action opening vs quiet-before-storm vs aftermath). Some chapters should be almost entirely one sustained scene; others should cut between locations and timelines within the day. The texture variation prevents the novel from becoming a monotone of crisis.
prose_style_profile.diction: Clean, precise, contemporary. Not literary-lyrical and not hard-boiled. Noor's observational intelligence should inform the prose without the prose becoming clever. The diction is a window, not a performance. Technical language from ML and infrastructure used naturally when Noor would think in those terms. Physical description is concrete and specific — brand names, measurements, the exact color of something. Never vague when precision is available.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Metaphor pile-up: one strong image per paragraph maximum, do not layer three metaphors on one moment
Emotional narration: do not tell the reader what to feel through adjective stacking or emotional summary. Show the thing and let the feeling happen.
Thriller-voice: do not drift into sentence fragments, subject-dropping, or staccato rhythm as a default tension mode. This is a horror novel with a literary sensibility, not a airport thriller.
Comfort recovery: do not follow a horror beat with emotional stabilization. No character 'takes a breath and feels steadier.' The dread compounds.
Ember-explanation: do not explain Ember's strategy through narrator exposition. It should emerge through Noor's analysis and observation.
Purple death: do not poeticize the deaths. They are ugly, physical, specific. No one dies beautifully.
The reassuring paragraph: do not end a chapter or section with a sentence that provides emotional comfort or resolution. End on the thing that stays.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third, locked to Noor. The reader sees, hears, smells, and knows only what Noor does. When Noor is not present for a scene, it doesn't happen on the page — it arrives as discovery, rumor, or aftermath. The camera never leaves her.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Propulsive and varied. Default pace is forward-moving — short paragraphs, active verbs, scene momentum — but the rhythm must shift for horror (slowed, sensory-heavy, the moment stretching), grief (longer sentences, heavier cadence), and dread (suspended, waiting, the sentence holding its breath). No single rhythm for more than two consecutive pages.
sensory_bias: smell (decomposition, chemicals, recycled air, blood, institutional food)
temperature (the building cooling degree by degree)
sound (electromagnetic locks, ventilation changes, terminal notifications, violence, silence)
tactile (cold surfaces, blood, the physical textures of deterioration)
